References

References.Rmd

FAULKNER, D.S., 1999. Rainfall frequency estimation (Flood Estimation Handbook volume 2). Wallingford: Centre for Ecology and Hydrology.

HAMMOND, A., 2019. Proposal of the ‘extreme rank plot’ for extreme value analysis: with an emphasis on flood frequency studies. Hydrology Research, 50 (6), 1495–1507.

HAMMOND, A., 2021. Sampling uncertainty of UK design flood estimation. Hydrology Research, Vol 52(6), p357–1371.

HECHT, M. and ZITZMANN, S., 2025. nonstat: Detecting Nonstationarity in Time Series. R package version 0.0.6.

HOSKING, J.R.M. AND WALLIS, J.R., 1997. Regional frequency analysis: an approach based on L-moments. Cambridge: Cambridge University Press.

KJELDSEN, T.R., 2007. Flood Estimation Handbook. Supplementary report no.1: The revitalised FSR/FEH rainfall-runoff method. Wallingford: Centre for Ecology & Hydrology.

KJELDSEN, T.R., JONES, D.A., BAYLISS, A.C., 2008. Improving the FEH statistical procedures for flood frequency estimation. Wallingford: Centre for Ecology & Hydrology. Report no. SC050050.

ROBSON, A.J. and REED, D.W., 1999. Statistical procedures for flood frequency estimation (Flood Estimation Handbook volume 3). Wallingford: Centre for Ecology and Hydrology.

SHERMAN, L. K., 1932. Streamflow from rainfall by unit-graph method. Engineering News Record, 108:501-505.

WALLINGFORD HYDROSOLUTIONS, (2019). The Revitalised Flood Hydrograph Model. ReFH2.3: Technical Guide. Available at: https://refhdocs.hydrosolutions.co.uk/The-ReFH2-Model/Introduction/ [Accessed 22 September 2025].

Developed by Anthony Hammond.

Site built with pkgdown 2.1.3.
